Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: Now that we have launched our attack using the SS-N-3 missiles, let's look at some of the midcourse guidance features of this missile.

As you click on each outbound missile, you can see the missile's midcourse guidance status on the bottom bar 'Status' field. Missiles that have maintained guidance will show their target track and their link status, and can be manually retargeted by selecting them on the Tactical Display and then right clicking a new target track or area of the map. You can also use this capability to fire two groups of missiles and then have them converge on the target from different bearings.
Note: SS-N-3's midcourse guidance capability will transfer radar seeker data back to the firing platform, so these missiles can be used as impromptu recon drones.
